title: Stave Finding Contract v1
description: Contract schema for individual Stave findings. Each finding represents a single control violation detected for a specific asset.

control_id.title: Control ID
control_id.description: Unique identifier of the control that was violated (e.g., CTL.STORAGE.PUBLIC.001).

control_name.title: Control Name
control_name.description: Human-readable name of the violated control.

control_description.title: Control Description
control_description.description: Detailed description of the unsafe condition detected by the control.

asset_id.title: Asset ID
asset_id.description: Unique identifier of the asset that violated the control.

asset_type.title: Asset Type
asset_type.description: Type of the asset (e.g., storage_bucket, iam_role).

asset_vendor.title: Asset Vendor
asset_vendor.description: Cloud vendor or platform of the asset (e.g., aws, gcp, azure).

source.title: Source Reference
source.description: Optional reference to the source file and line where the asset was defined.

evidence.title: Violation Evidence
evidence.description: Temporal and causal evidence supporting this finding.

remediation.title: Remediation Guidance
remediation.description: Guidance for remediating this specific violation.

fix_plan.title: Fix Plan
fix_plan.description: Machine-readable deterministic fix plan for supported finding classes.

exposure.title: Exposure Classification
exposure.description: Classifies who can reach the asset and how.

type.title: Exposure Type
type.description: Kind of exposure (e.g., public_read, public_write, resource_takeover).

principal_scope.title: Principal Scope
principal_scope.description: Audience that gains access (e.g., global, authenticated, cross_account).

posture_drift.title: Posture Drift Classification
posture_drift.description: Classifies the temporal behavior pattern of the violation, computed from the asset timeline.

pattern.title: Drift Pattern
pattern.description: persistent = unsafe since first observation; degraded = was safe, now unsafe; intermittent = toggled between safe and unsafe.

episode_count.title: Episode Count
episode_count.description: Total number of unsafe episodes (closed + currently open).

sourceRef.title: Source Reference
sourceRef.description: Reference to a source file and line for traceability.

line.title: Source Line Number
line.description: Line number in the source file.

evidence.title: Temporal Evidence
evidence.description: Temporal evidence proving the duration and nature of the unsafe condition.

first_seen_unsafe.title: First Seen Unsafe
first_seen_unsafe.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) of the earliest observation snapshot where the asset was in an unsafe state.

unsafe_duration_hours.title: Unsafe Duration Hours
unsafe_duration_hours.description: Number of hours the asset has been continuously unsafe, computed from first_seen_unsafe to the evaluation time.

threshold_hours.title: Threshold Hours
threshold_hours.description: The max-unsafe threshold (in hours) that was exceeded to trigger this violation.

reason.title: Violation Reason
reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the asset is considered unsafe (e.g., which predicate rule matched).

value.title: Matched Field Value
value.description: The actual field value that triggered the predicate match. Type varies by control.

source_evidence.title: Snapshot Source Evidence
source_evidence.description: Per-snapshot source references showing where the unsafe state was observed across time.

sourceEvidence.title: Snapshot Source Evidence
sourceEvidence.description: Source reference for a specific observation snapshot that contributed to the finding.

snapshot_index.title: Snapshot Index
snapshot_index.description: Zero-based index of the observation snapshot in chronological order.

captured_at.title: Snapshot Timestamp
captured_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) when this snapshot was captured.

source.title: Source Reference
source.description: Source file and line reference for this snapshot's asset definition.

remediation.title: Remediation Guidance
remediation.description: Remediation guidance for resolving this violation.

description.title: Remediation Description
description.description: Human-readable explanation of how to fix the unsafe condition.

action.title: Remediation Action
action.description: Concrete remediation action (e.g., a CLI command, API call, or configuration change).

example.title: Remediation Example
example.description: Optional example showing the remediation action applied to a real asset.
